Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Responses To First Demand For Inspection And Production Ofo Documents And Things From Pltf Rae Dunham-Twells
Off Calendar Dec-19-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES:
Marvin K. Anderson, Esq., for defendant and moving party Pacific Bell Telephone Company
Kenneth Charles Green, Esq., for plaintiff and opposing party

HEARING HELD. TENTATIVE RULING IS ADOPTED. Defendant Pacific Bell Telephone Company's motion to compel further responses to request for production of documents is granted in part.
16
Granted in part. The request is modified to require production of documents that refer or relate to business activity at the Pine Street location such as existing equipment and lines, operating costs, lease and maintenance payments, customer service contracts, billable revenue, telephone costs, sales documents, and consulting and technical support fees.
17
Moot in light of agreement to produce all non-privileged documents. If plaintiff has not already done so, responsive documents shall be provided within 7 days of notice of this order.
20, 23, 30, 31
Granted. The objections are overruled and stricken.
Plaintiff is ordered to produce responsive documents shall be provided within 7 days of notice of this order. Parties further agreed to complete site inspection and produce documents by January 31, 2008 and that deposition of plaintiff's person most knowledgeable will proceed only after completion of depositions of plaintiffs. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN. Not reported.
